questions.q: What was the main objective of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920?
questions.answer: The main objective of the Non-Cooperation Movement was to achieve Swaraj through non-violent methods.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The British government, in order to counter the Indian National Congress, formed the _______________ in 1909.
What was the significance of the Chauri Chaura incident in the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.answer: The Chauri Chaura incident marked a setback for the Non-Cooperation Movement as it led to the death of 22 police personnel and the eventual withdrawal of the movement by Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Simon Commission, which was sent to India in 1927 to review the Government of India Act?
Explain the concept of 'Swaraj' as envisioned by Mahatma Gandhi.
questions.answer: Swaraj, as envisioned by Mahatma Gandhi, was a self-governing India that was free from British rule. It was not just political independence but also economic and social freedom. Gandhi believed that Swaraj could be achieved through non-violent means and was a moral and spiritual concept.

questions.q: True/False: The Government of India Act of 1935 was a major step towards the attainment of independence by India.
What was the main cause of the failure of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.answer: The main cause of the failure of the Non-Cooperation Movement was the Chauri Chaura incident and the subsequent withdrawal of the movement by Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Who was the President of the Indian National Congress at the time of the Quit India Movement in 1942?
Explain the significance of the Non-Cooperation Movement in the Indian freedom struggle.
questions.answer: The Non-Cooperation Movement was a significant event in the Indian freedom struggle as it was the first major mass movement against British rule. The movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 and involved widespread civil disobedience and non-violent resistance. The movement was a major success in terms of mobilizing public opinion against British rule and was seen as a major step towards the attainment of independence.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Indian National Congress, at its Lahore Session in 1929, declared _______________ as its goal.
